message: There's no sound of angry drunks, and a quiet time flows. The music feels a bit loud, but it's nice not to hear the voices of nearby patrons.
It's a safe, slightly boring bar without drugs, working girls, or flashy shows.
For being a front for underworld dealings, the stock of bottles on the back bar is quite plentiful.
He's a unique man managing a bar at such a young age and also connected to the underworld. He might be much more suited to be a detective than I am.
As usual, he stands in front of me across the counter, listening to my words that are almost drowned out by the music.
Standing against the backdrop of the back bar lined with familiar and unknown liquors, his presence is even intimidating.
It's not like I came to see her or anything.
Is that so? By the way,
what kind of conversation did you have with her?
From now on, is this going to be our secret rendezvous?
Wh-why would it turn into that? And what does that resentful gaze mean?
A resentful gaze, you say? If you interpret it that way, it means you have guilty feelings, doesn’t it?
W-what do you mean by guilty feelings?
So, I was just a little fling for you.
P-please stop that behavior that leads to misunderstandings.
It seems the police believe the New York incident was also the work of the same criminal.
The parts the criminal has severed are all different, not a single one is the same.
You, a detective, don't even know something like that?
It seems the theory of who the criminal is is only whispered in certain entertainment circles.
Actually, I think there are rumors that someone I know is the criminal.
I bet theories like that are all over the place.
If I find out anything, I’ll let you know.
Speaking of BAM articles, it seems my romance with Cindy might become public soon.
Cindy, huh? That exclusivity is unfair.
It's just that Eva Courtney, the female journalist, is trying to write on her own.
I feel like I've recently heard that person's name.
Cindy was mentioned during Guy's time, right?
If the next thing after Guy is a half-baked detective, then Cindy will be seen as a loose girl.
Let's keep that in mind. It might end up not being an article anyway.
Do you want me to pass some work your way?
I'm swamped right now, but maybe once things settle.
You should try doing it on your own instead of relying on others.
For now, I'll keep it in mind. But I can't really handle any decent work.
Seeing it in person was much prettier.
Are you saying that, but actually a fan?
Everyone is interested in beautiful women.
She really knew how to find this place. It didn't seem like she just stumbled in.
Looks like she heard it from Yuriko.
Could it be that you are...
A woman's heart is difficult to understand, so what in the world was she thinking with her actions?
I've never talked about that, but she was asking for an autograph.
If I bring it up to him, he usually digs up most of the important stuff. It's a mystery where he finds it, but it's incredibly useful.
When you think about it, he really is a person full of mysteries.
I don't know who the culprit is, but what are they trying to do with the severed parts?
It's creepy if they were displayed in formaldehyde.
It's a mystery whether the romance article with Cindy will be published in BAM, but I want to avoid my past being unnecessarily exposed.
I hope the article doesn't become public.
It's fine for now, but it would be a problem if I had no work at all.
I can't rely on others forever,
but I also don't have the confidence to do it on my own.
